questions.question: Define onto (surjective) function with an example from R to R.
keyPoints: Definition: f: A→B is onto if ∀b∈B, ∃a∈A such that f(a)=b
Example: f(x) = x+1 from R to R is onto because for any y, x = y-1 gives f(x)=y
Alternative example: f(x)=2x is onto R to R, or f(x)=x³

questions.question: If |A| = 5 and |B| = 3, can an onto function exist from A to B? Justify.
keyPoints: Yes, because 5 ≥ 3
By pigeonhole principle, 5 domain elements can map to cover all 3 codomain elements
Number of onto functions = 3! × S(5,3) = 6 × 25 = 150

questions.question: Prove that composition of two onto functions is onto.
keyPoints: Let f: A→B and g: B→C be onto
Take arbitrary c ∈ C
Since g is onto, ∃b ∈ B with g(b) = c
Since f is onto, ∃a ∈ A with f(a) = b
Then (g∘f)(a) = g(f(a)) = g(b) = c
Therefore g∘f is onto

questions.question: What is the necessary and sufficient condition for a function to have a right inverse?
keyPoints: A function f: A→B has a right inverse iff f is onto
Right inverse g: B→A satisfies f(g(b)) = b for all b∈B
Proof: If f has right inverse, then for any b∈B, let a=g(b), then f(a)=b, so b is in range, hence f is onto
Conversely, if f is onto, for each b∈B choose some a∈A with f(a)=b, define g(b)=a, then f∘g = identity on B

questions.question: Show that f: R → [0,∞) given by f(x) = x² is onto but not one-one.
keyPoints: Onto proof: For any y ≥ 0, let x = √y (or -√y), then f(x) = x² = y
Since every non-negative number has a preimage, f is onto
Not one-one proof: f(2) = 4 and f(-2) = 4, but 2 ≠ -2
Thus different domain elements map to same codomain element, not one-one

questions.question: Find the number of onto functions from a set with 4 elements to a set with 2 elements.
keyPoints: Using inclusion-exclusion: Total functions = 2⁴ = 16
Subtract functions missing first codomain element: 1⁴ = 1
Subtract functions missing second codomain element: 1⁴ = 1
Add back functions missing both (constant functions): 1
Alternatively: 2! × S(4,2) = 2 × 7 = 14 (Wait - recalculate)
Let me recalculate: S(4,2) = 7 (partitions of 4 into 2 non-empty subsets)
Ways to assign 2 codomain elements to these 7 partitions: 2! = 2
So total = 7 × 2 = 14

questions.question: Explain why f: Z → Z defined by f(x) = 2x is not onto. What is its range?
keyPoints: f(x) = 2x maps integers to even integers only
Odd integers (1, 3, 5, ...) in codomain Z have no preimage
Range of f = {2x : x ∈ Z} = set of all even integers = 2Z
Since range ≠ codomain, function is not onto
To make it onto, codomain should be changed to 2Z (the even integers)
